api/spec/packages/aip/src/features.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Features domain: feature models (meter-backed with optional unit costs), cost query types, and CRUD+query operations compiled to v3 OpenAPI and Go/JS/Python SDKs.

patterns.name: Namespace isolation
patterns.description: All types and interfaces are declared inside `namespace Features;`. Every .tsp file opens with this namespace declaration to keep generated names scoped.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for polymorphic types
patterns.description: Use `@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })` on unions (e.g. FeatureUnitCost) so the generated OpenAPI uses a `type` field as discriminator without extra envelope wrapping.
patterns.example:
@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })
union FeatureUnitCost { manual: FeatureManualUnitCost, llm: FeatureLlmUnitCost }

patterns.name: Visibility annotations control lifecycle access
patterns.description: Use `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create)` etc. on fields to control which HTTP operations expose or accept a field. Read-only computed fields use `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)` only.

patterns.name: Extension markers for private/unstable/internal endpoints
patterns.description: Operations that are not yet public must carry all three extensions: `@extension(Shared.PrivateExtension, true)`, `@extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true)`, `@extension(Shared.InternalExtension, true)`.

patterns.name: Shared generic response wrappers
patterns.description: Operations return `Shared.PagePaginatedResponse<T>`, `Shared.CreateResponse<T>`, `Shared.UpdateResponse<T>`, `Shared.DeleteResponse` — never inline response shapes.

patterns.name: index.tsp as the re-export barrel
patterns.description: Each sub-folder has an index.tsp that imports all sibling .tsp files and re-opens the namespace so callers only need `import "./features/index.tsp"`.

patterns.name: Suppress linter rules with inline justification
patterns.description: When a field name pattern legitimately violates the `@openmeter/api-spec-aip` linter (e.g. nullable fields, repeated-prefix grouping), suppress with `#suppress` and a quoted reason string.

key_file_guides.role: Declares the FeatureOperations and FeatureCostOperations interfaces with all HTTP verbs, route params, and response types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and use `using TypeSpec.Http;` — omitting this breaks @query/@get/@post decorators. deepObject+explode style required for filter params.

key_file_guides.role: Defines FeatureUnitCost discriminated union (manual/llm), enums, and resolved pricing model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mutually exclusive fields (e.g. provider vs provider_property) are enforced by docs only — no TypeSpec constraint; add #suppress if linter flags repeated-prefix naming.

key_file_guides.role: Core Feature model using Shared.ResourceWithKey spread and optional meter + unit_cost fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FeatureUpdateRequest only includes updatable fields (unit_cost); do not add read-only fields to it.

key_file_guides.role: FeatureCostQueryRow and FeatureCostQueryResult models for the cost query endpoint; reuses Meters.MeterQueryRequest as request body.
key_file_guides.watch_for: cost field is nullable (Shared.Numeric | null) — requires #suppress on no-nullable rule.

anti_patterns: Declaring HTTP routes directly in model files — routes belong in operations.tsp only
Defining response envelope shapes inline instead of using Shared.CreateResponse<T>/PagePaginatedResponse<T>
Adding @visibility(Lifecycle.Create) to computed/server-set fields (e.g. id, created_at, status)
Omitting all three extension markers on internal-only operations
Editing generated api/v3/api.gen.go instead of regenerating after TypeSpec changes

decisions.decision: LLM unit cost uses a flat model with mutually exclusive static/property fields rather than a nested union
decisions.rationale: Keeps the API surface flat and JSON-friendly; TypeSpec discriminated unions can't enforce mutual exclusion so the constraint is documented and validated at the Go service layer.
Cost can be unresolvable when LLM pricing data is unavailable; null is the correct semantic rather than omitting the field, even though it violates the no-nullable linter rule.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new operation to FeatureOperations in operations.tsp

api/spec/packages/aip/src/invoices.purpose: TypeSpec type definitions for invoice-related models (InvoiceNumber scalar, BillingParty, tax identity, addresses) shared across billing operations in the v3 API.

patterns.name: Namespace scoping
patterns.description: All types live in `namespace Invoices;`. Files that only define types (no operations) still declare the namespace.
patterns.example:
namespace Invoices;
@friendlyName("BillingInvoiceNumber") scalar InvoiceNumber extends string;

patterns.name: Scalar types for domain-constrained strings
patterns.description: Use `scalar X extends string` with `@minLength`/`@maxLength`/`@example` for strongly-typed domain identifiers like InvoiceNumber.

patterns.name: Visibility on every mutable field
patterns.description: Every field on party models explicitly declares `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update)` or a subset — no implicit all-lifecycle fields.

patterns.name: friendlyName for SDK type naming
patterns.description: Use `@friendlyName("Billing<Name>")` to control the generated SDK type name and avoid collisions with Shared namespace types.

key_file_guides.role: Defines BillingParty, BillingPartyAddresses, BillingPartyTaxIdentity — the supplier/customer party models used on invoices.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Many intentionally omitted GOBL fields are documented in comments (identities, people, inboxes, emails, websites, telephones, registration, logos, ext) — do not add them without design discussion.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the InvoiceNumber scalar type. Billing invoice body models live in the billing sub-folder, not here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: This file only defines the InvoiceNumber scalar — do not add full invoice models here; they belong in the billing/ namespace.

anti_patterns: Adding full invoice CRUD operations here — operations belong in the billing/ folder's operations.tsp
Adding GOBL fields that are documented as intentionally omitted (identities, logos, registration, ext, etc.)
Defining party types without @friendlyName — generates SDK names that clash with Shared namespace

decisions.decision: BillingParty is kept intentionally minimal vs full GOBL party spec
decisions.rationale: Many GOBL fields (identity codes, people, inboxes, websites, logos) are not needed for OpenMeter's billing use case and are explicitly excluded per inline comments in party.tsp.

api/spec/packages/aip/src/llmcost.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the LLM cost pricing domain: global synced price models, per-namespace override CRUD, and filter/pagination operations compiled to v3 OpenAPI.

patterns.name: Split interfaces by resource boundary
patterns.description: Separate `LLMCostPricesOperations` (global read-only prices) from `LLMCostOverridesOperations` (namespace-scoped CRUD) into distinct interfaces so Go generates separate handler groups.
patterns.example:
interface LLMCostPricesOperations { list_prices(...); get_price(...); }
interface LLMCostOverridesOperations { list_overrides(...); create_override(...); delete_override(...); }

patterns.name: deepObject filter params with explode
patterns.description: All filter parameters use `@query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true })` so filter[field][op]=value query strings deserialize correctly.

patterns.name: Read-only visibility for all Price response fields
patterns.description: The Price model fields all use `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read)` since prices are system-managed; OverrideCreate is a separate flat input model for writes.

patterns.name: Enum for controlled string values
patterns.description: Use `enum` (not union) for closed string sets with known members like PriceSource. Use `union` only when open-ended or when needing @summary per variant.

key_file_guides.role: Declares LLMCostPricesOperations and LLMCostOverridesOperations interfaces with all HTTP endpoints.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and use `using TypeSpec.Http;`. Sort parameter documented in list_prices but not list_overrides — keep consistent if extending.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Price, ModelPricing, Provider, Model, OverrideCreate models and PriceSource enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: OverrideCreate uses model_id (string) not a ULID — override creation is upsert semantics (unique per provider+model+currency), not idempotent by ID.

anti_patterns: Merging global price operations and override operations into a single interface
Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Create/Update) on Price model fields — prices are read-only responses; OverrideCreate is the write input
Omitting effective_from on OverrideCreate — it is required to establish when the override takes effect

decisions.decision: OverrideCreate is a flat input model separate from the Price response model
decisions.rationale: The write input (provider string, model_id, pricing) is structurally different from the full Price response which includes system-managed fields (id, source, created_at). Sharing one model would require complex visibility gymnastics.

api/spec/packages/aip/src/meters.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Meters domain: Meter model (aggregation config, event_type, dimensions), query request/result types, and CRUD+query operations for the v3 API.

patterns.name: union for open-ish enumerations
patterns.description: MeterAggregation and MeterQueryGranularity use `union` (not `enum`) with string literals so generated SDKs treat them as string unions rather than closed enums, supporting forward-compatibility.
patterns.example: union MeterAggregation { sum: "sum", count: "count", unique_count: "unique_count", avg: "avg", min: "min", max: "max", latest: "latest" }

patterns.name: sharedRoute for content-negotiated variants
patterns.description: The meter query endpoint has two operations (JSON and CSV) using `@sharedRoute` so both map to the same URL; content type is discriminated by `@header contentType` in the response.
patterns.example:
@post @operationId("query-meter") @sharedRoute
query(@path meterId: Shared.ULID, @body request: MeterQueryRequest): { @header contentType: "application/json"; @body _: MeterQueryResult; } | ...

patterns.name: @example for model documentation
patterns.description: Models with complex structure (Meter, MeterQueryRequest, MeterQueryRow, MeterQueryResult) include `@example(#{ ... })` decorators to provide concrete OpenAPI examples in the generated spec.

patterns.name: Separate query.tsp for read-only query types
patterns.description: MeterQueryRequest, MeterQueryResult, MeterQueryRow, MeterQueryFilters, and MeterQueryGranularity are isolated in query.tsp (not meter.tsp) so they can be imported by other namespaces (e.g. Features.FeatureCostOperations).

key_file_guides.role: Declares MetersOperations (CRUD) and MetersQueryOperations (query+queryCsv). query and queryCsv share the same operationId via @sharedRoute.
key_file_guides.watch_for: queryCsv intentionally omits @body on the request to avoid anyOf in generated OpenAPI — do not add request body back.

key_file_guides.role: Core Meter model. dimensions field is `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update)` — it is updatable unlike most meter fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: aggregation and event_type are Create+Read only (immutable after creation). value_property is optional for count aggregation.

key_file_guides.role: All meter query I/O types. MeterQueryFilters.dimensions uses Record<Shared.QueryFilterStringMapItem> for dimension filtering.
key_file_guides.watch_for: group_by_dimensions has @maxItems(100). dimensions in MeterQueryRow is Record<string> (output), while MeterQueryFilters.dimensions uses filter map items (input).

anti_patterns: Using `enum` instead of `union` for MeterAggregation — breaks forward-compatibility of generated SDKs
Adding request body to queryCsv — intentionally omitted to prevent anyOf in OpenAPI output
Putting query types (MeterQueryRequest etc.) in meter.tsp — they belong in query.tsp for cross-namespace reuse

decisions.decision: CSV export handled as a sharedRoute variant of the same POST endpoint rather than a separate GET endpoint
decisions.rationale: Content negotiation (Accept: text/csv) on a single URL is idiomatic REST; sharedRoute avoids duplicating route+operationId while allowing separate TypeSpec response type shapes.

api/spec/packages/aip/src/productcatalog.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the ProductCatalog domain: Plan (versioned phases with rate cards), Addon, PlanAddon associations, pricing models (free/flat/unit/graduated/volume), UnitConfig conversions, and CRUD+lifecycle operations compiled to v3 OpenAPI.

patterns.name: Versioned resources with status lifecycle
patterns.description: Plan and Addon use version (integer, Read-only, minValue(1)), effective_from/effective_to (Read-only), and a computed status enum (draft/active/archived/scheduled). Status is never set directly — derived from effective dates.

patterns.name: Lifecycle action sub-routes as @post operations
patterns.description: State transitions (publish, archive) are modeled as POST sub-routes (/{id}/publish, /{id}/archive) rather than PATCH fields, returning Shared.UpdateResponse<T>.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for Price polymorphism
patterns.description: Price and PriceUsageBased are `@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })` unions. Every variant model carries a `type` field matching the PriceType enum.
patterns.example:
@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })
union Price { free: PriceFree, flat: PriceFlat, unit: PriceUnit, graduated: PriceGraduated, volume: PriceVolume }

patterns.name: validation_errors as read-only list on resources
patterns.description: Plan, Addon, and PlanAddon all carry `@visibility(Lifecycle.Read) validation_errors?: ProductCatalogValidationError[]` to surface draft-state validation issues through the API.

patterns.name: Upsert operations use Shared.UpsertRequest<T> and Shared.UpsertResponse<T>
patterns.description: PUT operations on versioned resources (update-plan, update-addon, update-plan-addon) use UpsertRequest/UpsertResponse wrappers, not CreateRequest/UpdateResponse.
patterns.example: @put updatePlan(@path planId: Shared.ULID, @body plan: Shared.UpsertRequest<Plan>): Shared.UpsertResponse<Plan> | ...

patterns.name: PlanPhase uses PickProperties spread to reuse ResourceWithKey fields
patterns.description: PlanPhase spreads only selected fields from ResourceWithKey via `PickProperties<Shared.ResourceWithKey, "key" | "name" | "description" | "labels">` instead of the full spread.

key_file_guides.role: RateCard model (key, feature ref, billing_cadence, price, payment_term, commitments, discounts, tax_config). Imports Features namespace for feature reference.
key_file_guides.watch_for: billing_cadence nullable means one-time charge (only valid for flat prices). settlement_mode enum is defined here but may not be referenced on RateCard directly — check for usage.

key_file_guides.role: Price discriminated union and all variant models (PriceFree, PriceFlat, PriceUnit, PriceGraduated, PriceVolume) plus PriceTier and SpendCommitments.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PriceUsageBased is a sub-union (unit/graduated/volume only) used when a usage-based-only price type is needed. PriceTier requires at least one of flat_price or unit_price set (docs only, not enforced by TypeSpec).

key_file_guides.role: UnitConfig model for raw-to-billing-unit conversion and InvoiceUsageQuantityDetail for audit trail. Explains v1 DynamicPrice/PackagePrice equivalents.
key_file_guides.watch_for: rounding defaults to None; precision only meaningful when rounding != none. applied_unit_config on InvoiceUsageQuantityDetail is a snapshot (Read-only) for historical correctness.

key_file_guides.role: Plan and PlanPhase models, PlanStatus enum, ProductCatalogValidationError model reused across all catalog entities.
key_file_guides.watch_for: phases has @minItems(1) — at least one phase required. billing_cadence on Plan is Create+Read only (immutable).

key_file_guides.role: PlanOperations, AddonOperations, PlanAddonOperations interfaces. PlanAddon sub-resource routes nest under /{planId}/addons/.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All operations carry the three extension markers (Private/Unstable/Internal). PlanAddon operations require both @path planId and @path planAddonId.

anti_patterns: Setting status or effective_from/effective_to directly in Create/Update requests — they are server-computed Read-only fields
Using CreateRequest/UpdateResponse for PUT plan/addon operations — use UpsertRequest/UpsertResponse
Adding pricing logic (tier calculations, commitment enforcement) to TypeSpec models — belongs in Go billing service layer
Omitting validation_errors from new catalog resource models — required for draft-state UX
Using RateCard.price with PriceFree for usage-based rate cards — PriceUsageBased union restricts to unit/graduated/volume

decisions.decision: UnitConfig is a separate model on RateCard rather than embedded in price types
decisions.rationale: The same feature can be billed in different converted units across plans (bytes→GB, seconds→hours); keeping unit conversion at the rate card level decouples it from the pricing model and allows reuse.

decisions.decision: Plan/Addon versioning is server-managed (version field Read-only, incremented on update)
decisions.rationale: Prevents clients from accidentally creating version conflicts; the server controls the version chain ensuring subscription compatibility checks remain valid.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new lifecycle action (e.g. suspend-plan) to PlanOperations
